post.description:
Create a subscription programmatically.

This endpoint only allows to create subscription on free products.
For paid products, use the checkout flow.

No initial order will be created and no confirmation email will be sent.

**Scopes**: `subscriptions:write`

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.subscriptions.create(
    product_id='d8dd2de1-21b7-4a41-8bc3-ce909c0cfe23',
    customer_id='992fae2a-2a17-4b7a-8d9e-e287cf90131b',
)
print(response)

post.description: Create an OAuth2 client.

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.oauth2.clients.oauth2.create_client(
    redirect_uris=['https://example.com'],
    token_endpoint_auth_method='client_secret_post',
    grant_types=['authorization_code', 'refresh_token'],
    response_types=['code'],
    scope=('openid profile email user:read user:write organizations:read '
     'organizations:write custom_fields:read custom_fields:write '
     'discounts:read discounts:write checkout_links:read '
     'checkout_links:write checkouts:read checkouts:write '
     'transactions:read transactions:write payouts:read payouts:write '
     'products:read products:write benefits:read benefits:write '
     'events:read events:write meters:read meters:write files:read '
     'files:write subscriptions:read subscriptions:write customers:read '
     'customers:write members:read members:write wallets:read '
     'wallets:write disputes:read disputes:write customer_meters:read '
     'customer_sessions:write member_sessions:write customer_seats:read '
     'customer_seats:write orders:read orders:write refunds:read '
     'refunds:write payments:read metrics:read metrics:write webhooks:read '
     'webhooks:write license_keys:read license_keys:write '
     'customer_portal:read customer_portal:write notifications:read '
     'notifications:write notification_recipients:read '
     'notification_recipients:write'),
    client_name='string',
)
print(response)

get.description: Get an OAuth2 client by Client ID.

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.oauth2.clients.oauth2.get_client(
    'string',
)
print(response)

from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.oauth2.clients.oauth2.update_client(
    'string',
    redirect_uris=['https://example.com'],
    token_endpoint_auth_method='client_secret_post',
    grant_types=['authorization_code', 'refresh_token'],
    response_types=['code'],
    scope=('openid profile email user:read user:write organizations:read '
     'organizations:write custom_fields:read custom_fields:write '
     'discounts:read discounts:write checkout_links:read '
     'checkout_links:write checkouts:read checkouts:write '
     'transactions:read transactions:write payouts:read payouts:write '
     'products:read products:write benefits:read benefits:write '
     'events:read events:write meters:read meters:write files:read '
     'files:write subscriptions:read subscriptions:write customers:read '
     'customers:write members:read members:write wallets:read '
     'wallets:write disputes:read disputes:write customer_meters:read '
     'customer_sessions:write member_sessions:write customer_seats:read '
     'customer_seats:write orders:read orders:write refunds:read '
     'refunds:write payments:read metrics:read metrics:write webhooks:read '
     'webhooks:write license_keys:read license_keys:write '
     'customer_portal:read customer_portal:write notifications:read '
     'notifications:write notification_recipients:read '
     'notification_recipients:write'),
    client_name='string',
    client_id='string',
)
print(response)

from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

polar.oauth2.clients.oauth2.delete_client(
    'string',
)

from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

print(response)

post.description:
Create a draft order for an off-session charge against a saved payment
method. The order is created with `status=draft` and no invoice number;
call `POST /v1/orders/{id}/finalize` to attempt the charge.

The organization must have the `off_session_charges_enabled` feature flag.

**Scopes**: `orders:write`

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.orders.create(
    customer_id='00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000',
    product_id='00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000',
)
print(response)

post.description:
Finalize a draft order and synchronously attempt an off-session charge.

On success, the order transitions to `paid` and benefit grants fire
before the response returns. On failure (decline, missing payment method,
SCA challenge), the order stays in `draft` and a 4xx error is returned.

The request fails with 412 if the order is not in `draft` status.

**Scopes**: `orders:write`

402.description: The charge failed, or requires customer authentication (e.g. a 3DS challenge) that can't be completed off-session.

403.description: Off-session charges are not enabled for this organization, or its account can't currently accept payments.

412.description: The order is not in `draft` status.

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.orders.finalize(
    '00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000',
)
print(response)

post.description:
Trigger generation of an order's invoice.

**Scopes**: `orders:read`

409.description: Order is not eligible for invoice generation (invalid status).

422.description: Order is missing billing name or address.

x-mint.content:
<Warning>
  Once the invoice is generated, it's permanent and cannot be modified.

  Make sure the billing details (name and address) are correct before generating the invoice. You can update them before generating the invoice by calling the [`PATCH /v1/orders/{id}`](/api-reference/orders/update-order) endpoint.
</Warning>

<Note>
  After successfully calling this endpoint, you get a `202` response, meaning the generation of the invoice has been scheduled. It usually only takes a few seconds before you can retrieve the invoice using the [`GET /v1/orders/{id}/invoice`](/api-reference/orders/get-order-invoice) endpoint.

  If you want a reliable notification when the invoice is ready, you can listen to the [`order.updated`](/api-reference/orderupdated) webhook and check the [`is_invoice_generated` field](/api-reference/orderupdated#schema-data-is-invoice-generated).
</Note>

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.orders.generate_invoice(
    '00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000',
)
print(response)

get.description:
Get an order's invoice data.

**Scopes**: `orders:read`

x-mint.content:
<Note>
  The invoice must be generated first before it can be retrieved. You should call the [`POST /v1/orders/{id}/invoice`](/api-reference/orders/generate-order-invoice) endpoint to generate the invoice.

  If the invoice is not generated, you will receive a `404` error.
</Note>

<Warning>
  Looking to create a single use checkout session? Checkout Links are probably **not** what you're looking for.

  Checkout Links are shareable links that generate checkout sessions when opened. They are very handy to start a purchase from your website or social media.

  However, if you want to start a checkout for one of your user **inside** your product, you should use the [Checkout Sessions API](/api-reference/checkouts/create-checkout-session).
</Warning>

delete.description:
Delete a customer.

This action cannot be undone and will immediately:
- Cancel any active subscriptions for the customer
- Revoke all their benefits
- Clear any `external_id`

Use it only in the context of deleting a user within your
own service. Otherwise, use more granular API endpoints to cancel
a specific subscription or revoke certain benefits.

Note: The customers information will nonetheless be retained for historic
orders and subscriptions.

Set `anonymize=true` to also anonymize PII for GDPR compliance.

**Scopes**: `customers:write`

parameters.name: anonymize

schema.description: If true, also anonymize the customer's personal data for GDPR compliance. This replaces email with a hashed version, hashes name and billing name (name preserved for businesses with tax_id), clears billing address, and removes OAuth account data.
schema.title: Anonymize

parameters.description: If true, also anonymize the customer's personal data for GDPR compliance. This replaces email with a hashed version, hashes name and billing name (name preserved for businesses with tax_id), clears billing address, and removes OAuth account data.

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

polar.customers.delete(
    '00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000',
    anonymize=False,
)

post.description:
Validate a license key.

> This endpoint doesn't require authentication and can be safely used on a public
> client, like a desktop application or a mobile app.
> If you plan to validate a license key on a server, use the `/v1/license-keys/validate`
> endpoint instead.

print(response)

post.description:
Activate a license key instance.

> This endpoint doesn't require authentication and can be safely used on a public
> client, like a desktop application or a mobile app.
> If you plan to validate a license key on a server, use the `/v1/license-keys/activate`
> endpoint instead.

403.description: License key activation not supported or limit reached. Use /validate endpoint for licenses without activations.

x-mint.content:
<Tip>
  You only need to use this endpoint if you have device **activations** enabled on the license key benefit. You then use this endpoint to reserve an allocation for a specific device. Store the unique activation ID from the response on the device and use it as extra validation in the [/validate](/api-reference/customer_portal/validate-license-key) endpoint.

  Not using **activations**? Just use the [/validate](/api-reference/customer_portal/validate-license-key) endpoint directly instead.
</Tip>

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.customer_portal.license_keys.activate(
    key='string',
    organization_id='00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000',
    label='string',
)
print(response)

post.description: Trigger generation of an order's invoice.

409.description: Order is not eligible for invoice generation (invalid status).

422.description: Order is missing billing name or address.

x-mint.content:
<Warning>
  Once the invoice is generated, it's permanent and cannot be modified.

  Make sure the billing details (name and address) are correct before generating the invoice. You can update them before generating the invoice by calling the [`PATCH /v1/customer-portal/orders/{id}`](/api-reference/customer_portal/update-order) endpoint.
</Warning>

<Note>
  After successfully calling this endpoint, you get a `202` response, meaning the generation of the invoice has been scheduled. It usually only takes a few seconds before you can retrieve the invoice using the [`GET /v1/customer-portal/orders/{id}/invoice`](/api-reference/customer_portal/get-order-invoice) endpoint.

  If you want a reliable notification when the invoice is ready, you can listen to the [`order.updated`](/api-reference/orderupdated) webhook and check the [`is_invoice_generated` field](/api-reference/orderupdated#schema-data-is-invoice-generated).
</Note>

x-codeSamples.source:
from polar.v2026_04 import Polar

polar = Polar("polar_oat_xxx")

response = polar.customer_portal.orders.generate_invoice(
    '00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000',
)
print(response)

  If the invoice is not generated, you will receive a `404` error.

post.description:
Sent when a new checkout is created.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a checkout is updated.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a checkout expires.

This event fires when a checkout reaches its expiration time without being completed.
Developers can use this to send reminder emails or track checkout abandonment.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a new customer is created.

A customer can be created:

* After a successful checkout.
* Programmatically via the API.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a customer is updated.

This event is fired when the customer details are updated.

If you want to be notified when a customer subscription or benefit state changes, you should listen to the `customer_state_changed` event.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a customer is deleted.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a customer state has changed.

It's triggered when:

* Customer is created, updated or deleted.
* A subscription is created or updated.
* A benefit is granted or revoked.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a new customer seat is assigned.

This event is triggered when a seat is assigned to a customer by the organization.
The customer will receive an invitation email to claim the seat.

Sent when a customer seat is claimed.

This event is triggered when a customer accepts the seat invitation and claims their access.

Sent when a customer seat is revoked.

This event is triggered when access to a seat is revoked, either manually by the organization or automatically when a subscription is canceled.

Sent when a new member is created.

A member represents an individual within a customer (team).
This event is triggered when a member is added to a customer,
either programmatically via the API or when an owner is automatically
created for a new customer.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a member is updated.

This event is triggered when member details are updated,
such as their name or role within the customer.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a member is deleted.

This event is triggered when a member is removed from a customer.
Any active seats assigned to the member will be automatically revoked.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a new order is created.

A new order is created when:

* A customer purchases a one-time product. In this case, `billing_reason` is set to `purchase`.
* A customer starts a subscription. In this case, `billing_reason` is set to `subscription_create`.
* A subscription is renewed. In this case, `billing_reason` is set to `subscription_cycle`.
* A subscription is upgraded or downgraded with an immediate proration invoice. In this case, `billing_reason` is set to `subscription_update`.

> [!WARNING]
> The order might not be paid yet, so the `status` field might be `pending`.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when an order is updated.

An order is updated when:

* Its status changes, e.g. from `pending` to `paid`.
* It's refunded, partially or fully.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when an order is paid.

When you receive this event, the order is fully processed and payment has been received.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when an order is fully or partially refunded.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a new subscription is created.

When this event occurs, the subscription `status` might not be `active` yet, as we can still have to wait for the first payment to be processed.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a subscription is updated. This event fires for all changes to the subscription, including renewals.

If you want more specific events, you can listen to `subscription.active`, `subscription.canceled`, `subscription.past_due`, and `subscription.revoked`.

To listen specifically for renewals, you can listen to `order.created` events and check the `billing_reason` field.

**Discord & Slack support:** On cancellation, past due, and revocation. Renewals are skipped.

Sent when a subscription becomes active,
whether because it's a new paid subscription or because payment was recovered.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a subscription is canceled.
Customers might still have access until the end of the current period.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a customer revokes a pending cancellation.

When a customer cancels with "at period end", they retain access until the
subscription would renew. During this time, they can change their mind and
undo the cancellation. This event is triggered when they do so.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a subscription is revoked and the user loses access immediately.
Happens when the subscription is canceled or payment retries are exhausted (status becomes `unpaid`).

For payment failures that can still be recovered, see `subscription.past_due`.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a subscription payment fails and the subscription enters `past_due` status.

This is a recoverable state - the customer can update their payment method to restore the subscription.
Benefits may be revoked depending on the organization's grace period settings.

If payment retries are exhausted, a `subscription.revoked` event will be sent.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a subscription is paused and the customer temporarily loses access.

No order is created while paused. The subscription resumes either on its
scheduled resume date or when resumed manually, starting a new billing period.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a paused subscription resumes, restoring the customer's access.

Resuming starts a new billing period and charges the customer immediately.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a refund is created regardless of status.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a refund is updated.

**Discord & Slack support:** Full

Sent when a new product is created.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a product is updated.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a organization is updated.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a new benefit is created.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a benefit is updated.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a new benefit grant is created.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a benefit grant is updated.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a benefit grant is cycled,
meaning the related subscription has been renewed for another period.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

Sent when a benefit grant is revoked.

**Discord & Slack support:** Basic

label.title: Label
label.description: Button label.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Lowercase ISO code of the suggested currency, e.g. `eur`.

AddCurrencyAction.title: AddCurrencyAction
AddCurrencyAction.description:
A nudge to offer prices in an additional presentment currency.

The client routes to the products list, where per-product pricing can be
extended. Nothing is ever applied automatically.

label.title: Label
label.description: Button label.

product_id.title: Product Id
product_id.description: The product whose pricing to review.

current_price_amount.title: Current Price Amount
current_price_amount.description: The product's current list price, in cents.

suggested_price_amount.title: Suggested Price Amount
suggested_price_amount.description: Reference list price (cents) that would restore the target gross margin at the current cost to serve. Null when no meaningful suggestion can be computed.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: ISO currency code of the amounts.

AdjustPriceAction.title: AdjustPriceAction
AdjustPriceAction.description:
A recommendation to review a product's pricing, with a reference point.

The suggested amount is a *reference*, not a mandate: it's the list price
that would restore the target gross margin at the current cost to serve,
ignoring demand elasticity. It applies to new customers only — existing
subscriptions keep their price. The client routes to the product's pricing
editor; nothing is ever applied automatically.

CardPayment.title: CardPayment
CardPayment.description: Schema of a payment with a card payment method.

brand.title: Brand
brand.description: The brand of the card used for the payment.

last4.title: Last4
last4.description: The last 4 digits of the card number.

CardPaymentMetadata.description: Additional metadata for a card payment method.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

custom_field_data.title: Custom Field Data
custom_field_data.description: Key-value object storing custom field values.

status.description:
Status of the checkout session.

        - Open: the checkout session was opened.
        - Expired: the checkout session was expired and is no more accessible.
        - Confirmed: the user on the checkout session clicked Pay. This is not indicative of the payment's success status.
        - Failed: the checkout definitely failed for technical reasons and cannot be retried. In most cases, this state is never reached.
        - Succeeded: the payment on the checkout was performed successfully.

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: Client secret used to update and complete the checkout session from the client.

url.title: Url
url.description: URL where the customer can access the checkout session.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Expiration date and time of the checkout session.

success_url.title: Success Url
success_url.description: URL where the customer will be redirected after a successful payment.

return_url.title: Return Url
return_url.description: When set, a back button will be shown in the checkout to return to this URL.

embed_origin.title: Embed Origin
embed_origin.description: When checkout is embedded, represents the Origin of the page embedding the checkout. Used as a security measure to send messages only to the embedding page.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount in cents, before discounts and taxes.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Predefined number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

min_seats.title: Min Seats
min_seats.description: Minimum number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

max_seats.title: Max Seats
max_seats.description: Maximum number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

net_amount.title: Net Amount
net_amount.description: Amount in cents, after discounts but before taxes.

tax_amount.title: Tax Amount
tax_amount.description: Sales tax amount in cents. If `null`, it means there is no enough information yet to calculate it.

tax_behavior.description: Tax behavior of the checkout. `inclusive` means the price includes tax, `exclusive` means tax is added on top. If `null`, tax is not yet calculated.

total_amount.title: Total Amount
total_amount.description: Amount in cents, after discounts and taxes.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency code of the checkout session.

allow_trial.title: Allow Trial
allow_trial.description: Whether to enable the trial period for the checkout session. If `false`, the trial period will be disabled, even if the selected product has a trial configured.

active_trial_interval.description: Interval unit of the trial period, if any. This value is either set from the checkout, if `trial_interval` is set, or from the selected product.

active_trial_interval_count.title: Active Trial Interval Count
active_trial_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the trial period, if any. This value is either set from the checkout, if `trial_interval_count` is set, or from the selected product.

trial_end.title: Trial End
trial_end.description: End date and time of the trial period, if any.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: ID of the organization owning the checkout session.

product_id.title: Product Id
product_id.description: ID of the product to checkout.

product_price_id.title: Product Price Id
product_price_id.description: ID of the product price to checkout.

discount_id.title: Discount Id
discount_id.description: ID of the discount applied to the checkout.

allow_discount_codes.title: Allow Discount Codes
allow_discount_codes.description: Whether to allow the customer to apply discount codes. If you apply a discount through `discount_id`, it'll still be applied, but the customer won't be able to change it.

require_billing_address.title: Require Billing Address
require_billing_address.description: Whether to require the customer to fill their full billing address, instead of just the country. Customers in the US will always be required to fill their full address, regardless of this setting. If you preset the billing address, this setting will be automatically set to `true`.

is_discount_applicable.title: Is Discount Applicable
is_discount_applicable.description: Whether the discount is applicable to the checkout. Typically, free and custom prices are not discountable.

is_free_product_price.title: Is Free Product Price
is_free_product_price.description: Whether the product price is free, regardless of discounts.

is_payment_required.title: Is Payment Required
is_payment_required.description: Whether the checkout requires payment, e.g. in case of free products or discounts that cover the total amount.

is_payment_setup_required.title: Is Payment Setup Required
is_payment_setup_required.description: Whether the checkout requires setting up a payment method, regardless of the amount, e.g. subscriptions that have first free cycles.

is_payment_form_required.title: Is Payment Form Required
is_payment_form_required.description: Whether the checkout requires a payment form, whether because of a payment or payment method setup.

is_business_customer.title: Is Business Customer
is_business_customer.description: Whether the customer is a business or an individual. If `true`, the customer will be required to fill their full billing address and billing name.

billing_address_fields.description: Determine which billing address fields should be disabled, optional or required in the checkout form.

trial_interval.description: The interval unit for the trial period.

trial_interval_count.title: Trial Interval Count
trial_interval_count.description: The number of interval units for the trial period.

external_customer_id.title: External Customer Id
external_customer_id.description: ID of the customer in your system. If a matching customer exists on Polar, the resulting order will be linked to this customer. Otherwise, a new customer will be created with this external ID set.

products.title: Products
products.description: List of products available to select.

additionalProperties.description: List of prices for this product.

prices.title: Prices
prices.description: Mapping of product IDs to their list of prices.

product_price_id.title: Product Price Id

anyOf.description: Amount in cents, before discounts and taxes. Only useful for custom prices, it'll be ignored for fixed and free prices.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Number of seats for seat-based pricing.

anyOf.description: Locale of the customer, given as an IETF BCP 47 language tag, e.g. `en`, `en-US` or `en-GB-oxendict`. If `null` or unsupported, the locale will default to `en`.

visibility.description: The visibility of the product.

recurring_interval.description: The recurring interval of the product. If `None`, the product is a one-time purchase.

recurring_interval_count.title: Recurring Interval Count
recurring_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the subscription. If this is set to 1 the charge will happen every interval (e.g. every month), if set to 2 it will be every other month, and so on. None for one-time products.

meter_interval.description: The meter cycle of the product, independent of the billing interval. If `None`, metered concerns follow the billing interval.

meter_interval_count.title: Meter Interval Count
meter_interval_count.description: Number of meter interval units. None when no meter cycle is set.

is_recurring.title: Is Recurring
is_recurring.description: Whether the product is a subscription.

is_archived.title: Is Archived
is_archived.description: Whether the product is archived and no longer available.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: The ID of the organization owning the product.

prices.title: Prices
prices.description: List of prices for this product.

benefits.title: BenefitPublic
benefits.description: List of benefits granted by the product.

medias.title: Medias
medias.description: List of medias associated to the product.

CheckoutLinkProduct.title: CheckoutLinkProduct
CheckoutLinkProduct.description: Product data for a checkout link.

trial_interval.description: The interval unit for the trial period.

trial_interval_count.title: Trial Interval Count
trial_interval_count.description: The number of interval units for the trial period.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

products.title: Products
products.description: List of products that will be available to select at checkout.

allow_discount_codes.title: Allow Discount Codes
allow_discount_codes.description: Whether to allow the customer to apply discount codes. If you apply a discount through `discount_id`, it'll still be applied, but the customer won't be able to change it.

require_billing_address.title: Require Billing Address
require_billing_address.description: Whether to require the customer to fill their full billing address, instead of just the country. Customers in the US will always be required to fill their full address, regardless of this setting.

discount_id.title: Discount Id
discount_id.description: ID of the discount to apply to the checkout. If the discount is not applicable anymore when opening the checkout link, it'll be ignored.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Preconfigured number of seats for seat-based pricing. When set, checkout sessions created from this link are locked to this number of seats and the customer won't be able to change it. All products on the link must use seat-based pricing and allow this number of seats. If the products no longer accommodate this value when the link is opened, it'll be ignored.

success_url.title: Success Url
success_url.description: URL where the customer will be redirected after a successful payment.You can add the `checkout_id={CHECKOUT_ID}` query parameter to retrieve the checkout session id.

return_url.title: Return Url
return_url.description: When set, a back button will be shown in the checkout to return to this URL.

CheckoutLinkUpdate.description: Schema to update an existing checkout link.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

name.title: Name
name.description: Organization name shown in checkout, customer portal, emails etc.

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: Unique organization slug in checkout, customer portal and credit card statements.

avatar_url.title: Avatar Url
avatar_url.description: Avatar URL shown in checkout, customer portal, emails etc.

proration_behavior.description: Proration behavior applied when customer updates their subscription from the portal.

allow_customer_updates.title: Allow Customer Updates
allow_customer_updates.description: Whether customers can update their subscriptions from the customer portal.

allow_discount_codes.title: Allow Discount Codes
allow_discount_codes.description: Whether to allow the customer to apply discount codes. If you apply a discount through `discount_id`, it'll still be applied, but the customer won't be able to change it.

require_billing_address.title: Require Billing Address
require_billing_address.description: Whether to require the customer to fill their full billing address, instead of just the country. Customers in the US will always be required to fill their full address, regardless of this setting. If you preset the billing address, this setting will be automatically set to `true`.

anyOf.description: Amount in cents, before discounts and taxes. Only useful for custom prices, it'll be ignored for fixed and free prices.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Predefined number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

min_seats.title: Min Seats
min_seats.description: Minimum number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

max_seats.title: Max Seats
max_seats.description: Maximum number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

allow_trial.title: Allow Trial
allow_trial.description: Whether to enable the trial period for the checkout session. If `false`, the trial period will be disabled, even if the selected product has a trial configured.

customer_id.title: Customer Id
customer_id.description: ID of an existing customer in the organization. The customer data will be pre-filled in the checkout form. The resulting order will be linked to this customer.

is_business_customer.title: Is Business Customer
is_business_customer.description: Whether the customer is a business or an individual. If `true`, the customer will be required to fill their full billing address and billing name.

external_customer_id.title: External Customer Id
external_customer_id.description: ID of the customer in your system. If a matching customer exists on Polar, the resulting order will be linked to this customer. Otherwise, a new customer will be created with this external ID set.

customer_metadata.title: Customer Metadata
customer_metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information that'll be copied to the created customer.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

subscription_id.title: Subscription Id
subscription_id.description: ID of a subscription to upgrade. It must be on a free pricing. If checkout is successful, metadata set on this checkout will be copied to the subscription, and existing keys will be overwritten.

success_url.title: Success Url
success_url.description: URL where the customer will be redirected after a successful payment.You can add the `checkout_id={CHECKOUT_ID}` query parameter to retrieve the checkout session id.

return_url.title: Return Url
return_url.description: When set, a back button will be shown in the checkout to return to this URL.

embed_origin.title: Embed Origin
embed_origin.description: If you plan to embed the checkout session, set this to the Origin of the embedding page. It'll allow the Polar iframe to communicate with the parent page.

anyOf.description: Locale of the customer, given as an IETF BCP 47 language tag, e.g. `en`, `en-US` or `en-GB-oxendict`. If `null` or unsupported, the locale will default to `en`.

product_price_id.title: Product Price Id
product_price_id.description: ID of the product price to checkout.

products.title: Products
products.description: List of product IDs available to select at that checkout. The first one will be selected by default.

additionalProperties.description: List of prices for the product. At most one fixed price and one seat-based price may be combined (billed as `fixed + seat_charge`), or a single custom price may stand alone, plus any number of metered prices. A custom price cannot be combined with a fixed or seat-based price.

prices.title: Prices
prices.description: Optional mapping of product IDs to a list of ad-hoc prices to create for that product. If not set, catalog prices of the product will be used.

CheckoutProductsCreate.title: CheckoutProductsCreate
CheckoutProductsCreate.description:
Create a new checkout session from a list of products.
Customers will be able to switch between those products.

Metadata set on the checkout will be copied
to the resulting order and/or subscription.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

custom_field_data.title: Custom Field Data
custom_field_data.description: Key-value object storing custom field values.

status.description:
Status of the checkout session.

        - Open: the checkout session was opened.
        - Expired: the checkout session was expired and is no more accessible.
        - Confirmed: the user on the checkout session clicked Pay. This is not indicative of the payment's success status.
        - Failed: the checkout definitely failed for technical reasons and cannot be retried. In most cases, this state is never reached.
        - Succeeded: the payment on the checkout was performed successfully.

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: Client secret used to update and complete the checkout session from the client.

url.title: Url
url.description: URL where the customer can access the checkout session.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Expiration date and time of the checkout session.

success_url.title: Success Url
success_url.description: URL where the customer will be redirected after a successful payment.

return_url.title: Return Url
return_url.description: When set, a back button will be shown in the checkout to return to this URL.

embed_origin.title: Embed Origin
embed_origin.description: When checkout is embedded, represents the Origin of the page embedding the checkout. Used as a security measure to send messages only to the embedding page.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount in cents, before discounts and taxes.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Predefined number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

min_seats.title: Min Seats
min_seats.description: Minimum number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

max_seats.title: Max Seats
max_seats.description: Maximum number of seats (works with seat-based pricing only)

net_amount.title: Net Amount
net_amount.description: Amount in cents, after discounts but before taxes.

tax_amount.title: Tax Amount
tax_amount.description: Sales tax amount in cents. If `null`, it means there is no enough information yet to calculate it.

tax_behavior.description: Tax behavior of the checkout. `inclusive` means the price includes tax, `exclusive` means tax is added on top. If `null`, tax is not yet calculated.

total_amount.title: Total Amount
total_amount.description: Amount in cents, after discounts and taxes.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency code of the checkout session.

allow_trial.title: Allow Trial
allow_trial.description: Whether to enable the trial period for the checkout session. If `false`, the trial period will be disabled, even if the selected product has a trial configured.

active_trial_interval.description: Interval unit of the trial period, if any. This value is either set from the checkout, if `trial_interval` is set, or from the selected product.

active_trial_interval_count.title: Active Trial Interval Count
active_trial_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the trial period, if any. This value is either set from the checkout, if `trial_interval_count` is set, or from the selected product.

trial_end.title: Trial End
trial_end.description: End date and time of the trial period, if any.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: ID of the organization owning the checkout session.

product_id.description: ID of the product to checkout.

product_price_id.description: ID of the product price to checkout.

discount_id.description: ID of the discount applied to the checkout.

allow_discount_codes.description: Whether to allow the customer to apply discount codes. If you apply a discount through `discount_id`, it'll still be applied, but the customer won't be able to change it.

require_billing_address.title: Require Billing Address
require_billing_address.description: Whether to require the customer to fill their full billing address, instead of just the country. Customers in the US will always be required to fill their full address, regardless of this setting. If you preset the billing address, this setting will be automatically set to `true`.

is_free_product_price.title: Is Free Product Price
is_free_product_price.description: Whether the product price is free, regardless of discounts.

is_payment_required.title: Is Payment Required
is_payment_required.description: Whether the checkout requires payment, e.g. in case of free products or discounts that cover the total amount.

is_payment_setup_required.title: Is Payment Setup Required
is_payment_setup_required.description: Whether the checkout requires setting up a payment method, regardless of the amount, e.g. subscriptions that have first free cycles.

is_payment_form_required.title: Is Payment Form Required
is_payment_form_required.description: Whether the checkout requires a payment form, whether because of a payment or payment method setup.

is_business_customer.description: Whether the customer is a business or an individual. If `true`, the customer will be required to fill their full billing address and billing name.

prices.title: Prices
prices.description: Mapping of product IDs to their list of prices.

CheckoutPublicConfirmed.title: CheckoutPublicConfirmed
CheckoutPublicConfirmed.description:
Checkout session data retrieved using the client secret after confirmation.

It contains a customer session token to retrieve order information
right after the checkout.

custom_field_data.title: Custom Field Data
custom_field_data.description: Key-value object storing custom field values.

product_id.title: Product Id
product_id.description: ID of the product to checkout. Must be present in the checkout's product list.

product_price_id.title: Product Price Id
product_price_id.description: ID of the product price to checkout. Must correspond to a price present in the checkout's product list.

anyOf.description: Amount in cents, before discounts and taxes. Only useful for custom prices, it'll be ignored for fixed and free prices.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Number of seats for seat-based pricing.

anyOf.description: Locale of the customer, given as an IETF BCP 47 language tag, e.g. `en`, `en-US` or `en-GB-oxendict`. If `null` or unsupported, the locale will default to `en`.

trial_interval.description: The interval unit for the trial period.

trial_interval_count.title: Trial Interval Count
trial_interval_count.description: The number of interval units for the trial period.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

discount_id.title: Discount Id
discount_id.description: ID of the discount to apply to the checkout.

allow_discount_codes.title: Allow Discount Codes
allow_discount_codes.description: Whether to allow the customer to apply discount codes. If you apply a discount through `discount_id`, it'll still be applied, but the customer won't be able to change it.

require_billing_address.title: Require Billing Address
require_billing_address.description: Whether to require the customer to fill their full billing address, instead of just the country. Customers in the US will always be required to fill their full address, regardless of this setting. If you preset the billing address, this setting will be automatically set to `true`.

allow_trial.title: Allow Trial
allow_trial.description: Whether to enable the trial period for the checkout session. If `false`, the trial period will be disabled, even if the selected product has a trial configured.

anyOf.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

customer_metadata.title: Customer Metadata
customer_metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information that'll be copied to the created customer.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

success_url.title: Success Url
success_url.description: URL where the customer will be redirected after a successful payment.You can add the `checkout_id={CHECKOUT_ID}` query parameter to retrieve the checkout session id.

return_url.title: Return Url
return_url.description: When set, a back button will be shown in the checkout to return to this URL.

embed_origin.title: Embed Origin
embed_origin.description: If you plan to embed the checkout session, set this to the Origin of the embedding page. It'll allow the Polar iframe to communicate with the parent page.

CheckoutUpdate.title: CheckoutUpdate
CheckoutUpdate.description: Update an existing checkout session using an access token.

custom_field_data.title: Custom Field Data
custom_field_data.description: Key-value object storing custom field values.

product_id.title: Product Id
product_id.description: ID of the product to checkout. Must be present in the checkout's product list.

product_price_id.title: Product Price Id
product_price_id.description: ID of the product price to checkout. Must correspond to a price present in the checkout's product list.

anyOf.description: Amount in cents, before discounts and taxes. Only useful for custom prices, it'll be ignored for fixed and free prices.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Number of seats for seat-based pricing.

anyOf.description: Locale of the customer, given as an IETF BCP 47 language tag, e.g. `en`, `en-US` or `en-GB-oxendict`. If `null` or unsupported, the locale will default to `en`.

discount_code.title: Discount Code
discount_code.description: Discount code to apply to the checkout.

allow_trial.title: Allow Trial
allow_trial.description: Disable the trial period for the checkout session. It's mainly useful when the trial is blocked because the customer already redeemed one.

CheckoutUpdatePublic.title: CheckoutUpdatePublic
CheckoutUpdatePublic.description: Update an existing checkout session using the client secret.

ConfidenceLevel.title: ConfidenceLevel
ConfidenceLevel.description:
How much we trust an insight, derived from sample size and baseline variance.

Low-confidence insights are softened or suppressed so small merchants don't
get noise (e.g. "churn doubled" off a 1 -> 2 customer change).

external_id.description: The ID of the customer in your system. This must be unique within the organization. Once set, it can't be updated.

email.title: Email
email.description: The email address of the customer. This must be unique within the organization.

email_verified.title: Email Verified
email_verified.description: Whether the customer email address is verified. The address is automatically verified when the customer accesses the customer portal using their email address.

billing_name.title: Billing Name
billing_name.description: The name that should appear on the customer's invoices. Falls back to the customer name when not explicitly set.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: The ID of the organization owning the customer.

default_payment_method_id.title: Default Payment Method Id
default_payment_method_id.description: The ID of the customer's default payment method, if any. Use the payment methods endpoint to retrieve its details.

deleted_at.title: Deleted At
deleted_at.description: Timestamp for when the customer was soft deleted.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: The ID of the customer in your system. This must be unique within the organization. Once set, it can't be updated.

anyOf.description: Locale of the customer, given as an IETF BCP 47 language tag, e.g. `en`, `en-US` or `en-GB-oxendict`. If `null` or unsupported, the locale will default to `en`.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: The ID of the organization owning the customer. **Required unless you use an organization token.**

owner.description: Optional owner member to create with the customer. If not provided, an owner member will be automatically created using the customer's email and name.

email.title: Email
email.description: The email address of the customer. This must be unique within the organization.

modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

customer_id.title: Customer Id
customer_id.description: The ID of the customer.

balance.title: Balance
balance.description: The balance of the meter, i.e. the difference between credited and consumed units.

customer.description: The customer associated with this meter.

meter.description: The meter associated with this customer.

CustomerMeter.title: CustomerMeter
CustomerMeter.description: An active customer meter, with current consumed and credited units.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

paid.title: Paid
paid.description: Whether the order has been paid for.

subtotal_amount.title: Subtotal Amount
subtotal_amount.description: Amount in cents, before discounts and taxes.

net_amount.title: Net Amount
net_amount.description: Amount in cents, after discounts but before taxes.

total_amount.title: Total Amount
total_amount.description: Amount in cents, after discounts and taxes.

applied_balance_amount.title: Applied Balance Amount
applied_balance_amount.description: Customer's balance amount applied to this invoice. Can increase the total amount paid, if the customer has a negative balance,  or decrease it, if the customer has a positive balance.Amount in cents.

due_amount.title: Due Amount
due_amount.description: Amount in cents that is due for this order.

billing_name.title: Billing Name
billing_name.description: The name of the customer that should appear on the invoice.

invoice_number.title: Invoice Number
invoice_number.description: The invoice number associated with this order. `null` while the order is in `draft` status; assigned at finalize.

is_invoice_generated.title: Is Invoice Generated
is_invoice_generated.description: Whether an invoice has been generated for this order.

receipt_number.title: Receipt Number
receipt_number.description: The receipt number for this order. Set once the order is paid for organizations with receipts enabled. When set, a downloadable receipt PDF can be obtained via the receipt endpoint.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: Number of seats purchased (for seat-based one-time orders).

next_payment_attempt_at.title: Next Payment Attempt At
next_payment_attempt_at.description: When the next automatic payment retry is scheduled. `null` if the order is not in dunning or all retries have been exhausted.

description.title: Description
description.description: A summary description of the order.

refundable_amount.title: Refundable Amount
refundable_amount.description: Amount in cents that can still be refunded (net, before taxes). Accounts for any applied customer balance and previous refunds.

refundable_tax_amount.title: Refundable Tax Amount
refundable_tax_amount.description: Sales tax in cents that would be refunded if the full refundable amount is refunded.

confirmation_token_id.title: Confirmation Token Id
confirmation_token_id.description: ID of the Stripe confirmation token for new payment methods.

payment_method_id.title: Payment Method Id
payment_method_id.description: ID of an existing saved payment method.

CustomerOrderConfirmPayment.title: CustomerOrderConfirmPayment
CustomerOrderConfirmPayment.description: Schema to confirm a retry payment using either a saved payment method or a new confirmation token.

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: Client secret for handling additional actions.

CustomerOrderPaymentConfirmation.title: CustomerOrderPaymentConfirmation
CustomerOrderPaymentConfirmation.description: Response after confirming a retry payment.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

trial_interval.description: The interval unit for the trial period.

trial_interval_count.title: Trial Interval Count
trial_interval_count.description: The number of interval units for the trial period.

visibility.description: The visibility of the product.

recurring_interval.description: The recurring interval of the product. If `None`, the product is a one-time purchase.

recurring_interval_count.title: Recurring Interval Count
recurring_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the subscription. If this is set to 1 the charge will happen every interval (e.g. every month), if set to 2 it will be every other month, and so on. None for one-time products.

meter_interval.description: The meter cycle of the product, independent of the billing interval. If `None`, metered concerns follow the billing interval.

meter_interval_count.title: Meter Interval Count
meter_interval_count.description: Number of meter interval units. None when no meter cycle is set.

is_recurring.title: Is Recurring
is_recurring.description: Whether the product is a subscription.

is_archived.title: Is Archived
is_archived.description: Whether the product is archived and no longer available.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: The ID of the organization owning the product.

prices.title: Prices
prices.description: List of prices for this product.

benefits.title: BenefitPublic
benefits.description: List of benefits granted by the product.

medias.title: Medias
medias.description: List of medias associated to the product.

url.title: Url
url.description: The URL to the receipt PDF.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: The amount of the subscription.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: The currency of the subscription.

recurring_interval.description: The interval at which the subscription recurs.

recurring_interval_count.title: Recurring Interval Count
recurring_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the subscription. If this is set to 1 the charge will happen every interval (e.g. every month), if set to 2 it will be every other month, and so on.

current_period_start.title: Current Period Start
current_period_start.description: The start timestamp of the current billing period.

current_period_end.title: Current Period End
current_period_end.description: The end timestamp of the current billing period.

current_meter_period_start.title: Current Meter Period Start
current_meter_period_start.description: The start timestamp of the current meter period, if the product has a meter cycle set. Metered credits are granted and overage is settled on this cadence.

current_meter_period_end.title: Current Meter Period End
current_meter_period_end.description: The end timestamp of the current meter period, if the product has a meter cycle set. This is when credits next renew.

trial_start.title: Trial Start
trial_start.description: The start timestamp of the trial period, if any.

trial_end.title: Trial End
trial_end.description: The end timestamp of the trial period, if any.

cancel_at_period_end.title: Cancel At Period End
cancel_at_period_end.description: Whether the subscription will be canceled at the end of the current period.

canceled_at.title: Canceled At
canceled_at.description: The timestamp when the subscription was canceled. The subscription might still be active if `cancel_at_period_end` is `true`.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: The timestamp when the subscription started.

ends_at.title: Ends At
ends_at.description: The timestamp when the subscription will end.

ended_at.title: Ended At
ended_at.description: The timestamp when the subscription ended.

past_due_at.title: Past Due At
past_due_at.description: The timestamp when the subscription entered `past_due` status.

pause_at_period_end.title: Pause At Period End
pause_at_period_end.description: Whether the subscription will be paused at the end of the current period.

paused_at.title: Paused At
paused_at.description: The timestamp when the subscription was paused.

resumes_at.title: Resumes At
resumes_at.description: The timestamp when a paused subscription is scheduled to automatically resume, if set.

customer_id.title: Customer Id
customer_id.description: The ID of the subscribed customer.

product_id.title: Product Id
product_id.description: The ID of the subscribed product.

discount_id.title: Discount Id
discount_id.description: The ID of the applied discount, if any.

seats.title: Seats
seats.description: The number of seats for seat-based subscriptions. None for non-seat subscriptions.

billing_name.title: Billing Name
billing_name.description: The name of the customer that should appear on the invoice.

billing_address.description: The address of the customer that should appear on the invoice. Country and state fields cannot be updated.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

name.title: Name
name.description: Organization name shown in checkout, customer portal, emails etc.

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: Unique organization slug in checkout, customer portal and credit card statements.

avatar_url.title: Avatar Url
avatar_url.description: Avatar URL shown in checkout, customer portal, emails etc.

cancellation_reason.description:
Customers reason for cancellation.

* `too_expensive`: Too expensive for the customer.
* `missing_features`: Customer is missing certain features.
* `switched_service`: Customer switched to another service.
* `unused`: Customer is not using it enough.
* `customer_service`: Customer is not satisfied with the customer service.
* `low_quality`: Customer is unhappy with the quality.
* `too_complex`: Customer considers the service too complicated.
* `other`: Other reason(s).

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

name.description: Name of the discount. Will be displayed to the customer when the discount is applied.

code.title: Code
code.description: Code customers can use to apply the discount during checkout. Must be between 3 and 256 characters long and contain only alphanumeric characters.If not provided, the discount can only be applied via the API.

starts_at.title: Starts At
starts_at.description: Optional timestamp after which the discount is redeemable.

ends_at.title: Ends At
ends_at.description: Optional timestamp after which the discount is no longer redeemable.

max_redemptions.title: Max Redemptions
max_redemptions.description: Optional maximum number of times the discount can be redeemed.

anyOf.description: List of product IDs the discount can be applied to.

organization_id.title: Organization Id
organization_id.description: The ID of the organization owning the discount. **Required unless you use an organization token.**

duration.description: For subscriptions, determines if the discount should be applied once on the first invoice, forever, or for a certain number of months determined by `duration_in_months`.

duration_in_months.title: Duration In Months
duration_in_months.description:
Number of months the discount should be applied.

Required when `duration` is `repeating`. Must be omitted otherwise.

For this to work on yearly pricing, you should multiply this by 12.
For example, to apply the discount for 2 years, set this to 24.

anyOf.description: Fixed amount to discount from the invoice total.
The currency of the fixed amount discount.

additionalProperties.description: Fixed amount to discount from the invoice total.

anyOf.description: Map of currency to fixed amount to discount from the total. This allows specifying different discount amounts for different currencies.

status.description: Status of the dispute. `prevented` means we issued a refund before the dispute was escalated, avoiding any fees.

currency.title: Currency

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: The reason for the dispute as reported by the card network (e.g. `fraudulent`, `product_not_received`). `None` until the processor reports it.

evidence_due_by.title: Evidence Due By
evidence_due_by.description: Deadline to submit evidence in response to the dispute. `None` when no response is required.

past_due.title: Past Due
past_due.description: Whether the evidence submission deadline has passed.

order_id.title: Order Id
order_id.description: The ID of the order associated with the dispute.

payment_id.title: Payment Id
payment_id.description: The ID of the payment associated with the dispute.

case_id.description: The ID of the support case for this dispute, if one was opened.

type.description: The type of customer: 'individual' for single users, 'team' for customers with multiple members.

value.description: This driver's contribution in the metric's unit.

InsightDriver.title: InsightDriver
InsightDriver.description:
One contributor to the headline change.

Decomposition is what makes an insight *explain* rather than *announce* — e.g.
"MRR grew 12%, ~70% of it from Pro upgrades". Populated by a grouped query; the
MRR scaffold leaves this empty until a dedicated Tinybird breakdown pipe lands.

InsightSeverity.title: InsightSeverity
InsightSeverity.description:
How much the merchant should care, assigned per insight by its detector.

This is a property of what was *found*, not of the detector: the same MRR
detector emits `warning` when revenue falls and `info` when it grows. The
feed is ordered by severity first, so the most consequential reading always
leads.

OrganizationStartupProgramClaimRequest.title: OrganizationStartupProgramClaimRequest
OrganizationStartupProgramClaimRequest.description:
Inputs for claiming the Startup Program discount.

The checkout URLs are only used when the org is on the Free plan and
needs a checkout to set up a payment method; they're ignored on the
PATCH path (already-paid orgs).

OrganizationStartupProgramClaimResponse.title: OrganizationStartupProgramClaimResponse
OrganizationStartupProgramClaimResponse.description:
Result of claiming the Startup Program discount.

Exactly one field is set:
- ``checkout`` — Free → Scale: org needs to complete the checkout. The
  discount is already attached.
- ``subscription`` — Paid → Scale: the existing paid subscription was
  switched to Scale and the discount applied immediately.

subscription_id.title: Subscription Id
subscription_id.description: Polar subscription ID. Null when the organization has no active subscription and is on the free plan.

startup_program_status.title: Startup Program Status
startup_program_status.description: Polar Startup Program status for this organization. Derived from the organization's Startup Program discount: 'invited' when the discount exists and hasn't been redeemed, 'consumed' once it has. Null when the feature is disabled or the organization hasn't been invited.

startup_program_scale_product_id.title: Startup Program Scale Product Id
startup_program_scale_product_id.description: Polar product id of the Scale plan, against which the Startup Program discount applies. Null when the feature is disabled.

duration_in_months.description: Number of months the discount repeats; only set for 'repeating'.

basis_points.description: Percentage in basis points (10000 = 100%); only set for 'percentage'.

amounts.title: Amounts
amounts.description: Per-currency fixed amount in cents; only set for 'fixed'.

ends_at.title: Ends At
ends_at.description: Estimated date the discount stops applying. Computed for 'repeating' discounts from the subscription's started_at + duration_in_months; null for 'once' and 'forever' durations.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

prices.title: ProductPriceCreateList
prices.description: List of available prices for this product. It may combine at most one fixed price with one seat-based price (billed as `fixed + seat_charge`), or contain a single custom or free price, plus any number of metered prices. A free price cannot be combined with other prices, and a custom price cannot be combined with a fixed or seat-based price. Metered prices are not supported on one-time purchase products.

prices.description: List of available prices for this product. A free price cannot be combined with other prices, and a custom price cannot be combined with a fixed or seat-based price. Metered prices are not supported on one-time purchase products.

recurring_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the subscription. If this is set to 1 the charge will happen every interval (e.g. every month), if set to 2 it will be every other month, and so on.

meter_interval.description: Optional meter cycle, independent of the billing interval. When set, overage settlement, meter resets and meter-credit grants run on this cadence rather than the billing interval — e.g. yearly billing with monthly credits. It must evenly divide the billing interval. If `None`, metered concerns follow the billing interval. **Once set, it can't be changed.**

ProductPriceSeatTiers-Input.title: ProductPriceSeatTiers
ProductPriceSeatTiers-Input.description:
List of pricing tiers for seat-based pricing.

The minimum and maximum seat limits are derived from the tiers:
- minimum_seats = first tier's min_seats
- maximum_seats = last tier's max_seats (None for unlimited)

seat_tier_type.description: How tiers are applied. 'volume' prices all seats at the matching tier's rate. 'graduated' prices each tier's range independently.

minimum_seats.title: Minimum Seats
minimum_seats.description: Minimum number of seats required for purchase, derived from first tier.

maximum_seats.title: Maximum Seats
maximum_seats.description: Maximum number of seats allowed for purchase, derived from last tier. None for unlimited.

ProductPriceSeatTiers-Output.title: ProductPriceSeatTiers
ProductPriceSeatTiers-Output.description:
List of pricing tiers for seat-based pricing.

The minimum and maximum seat limits are derived from the tiers:
- minimum_seats = first tier's min_seats
- maximum_seats = last tier's max_seats (None for unlimited)

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

trial_interval.description: The interval unit for the trial period.

trial_interval_count.title: Trial Interval Count
trial_interval_count.description: The number of interval units for the trial period.

recurring_interval.description: The recurring interval of the product. If `None`, the product is a one-time purchase. **Can only be set on legacy recurring products. Once set, it can't be changed.**

recurring_interval_count.title: Recurring Interval Count
recurring_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the subscription. If this is set to 1 the charge will happen every interval (e.g. every month), if set to 2 it will be every other month, and so on. Once set, it can't be changed.**

is_archived.title: Is Archived
is_archived.description: Whether the product is archived. If `true`, the product won't be available for purchase anymore. Existing customers will still have access to their benefits, and subscriptions will continue normally.

visibility.description: The visibility of the product.

prices.title: Prices
prices.description: List of available prices for this product. If you want to keep existing prices, include them in the list as an `ExistingProductPrice` object.

medias.title: Medias
medias.description: List of file IDs. Each one must be on the same organization as the product, of type `product_media` and correctly uploaded.

anyOf.description: List of custom fields to attach.

occurrences.title: Occurrences
occurrences.description: Number of events with this property value.

customers.title: Customers
customers.description: Number of distinct customers associated with these events.

totals.title: Totals
totals.description: Sum of each aggregate field across all matching events.

PropertyGroupStat.title: PropertyGroupStat
PropertyGroupStat.description: Aggregate statistics for events grouped by a metadata property value.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount to refund in cents. Minimum is 1.

comment.title: Comment
comment.description: An internal comment about the refund.

revoke_benefits.title: Revoke Benefits
revoke_benefits.description:
Should this refund trigger the associated customer benefits to be revoked?

**Note:**
Only allowed in case the `order` is a one-time purchase.
Subscriptions automatically revoke customer benefits once the
subscription itself is revoked, i.e fully canceled.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

status.description: Status of the dispute. `prevented` means we issued a refund before the dispute was escalated, avoiding any fees.

resolved.title: Resolved
resolved.description: Whether the dispute has been resolved (won or lost).

closed.title: Closed
closed.description: Whether the dispute is closed (prevented, won, or lost).

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount in cents disputed.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency code of the dispute.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: The reason for the dispute as reported by the card network (e.g. `fraudulent`, `product_not_received`). `None` until the processor reports it.

evidence_due_by.title: Evidence Due By
evidence_due_by.description: Deadline to submit evidence in response to the dispute. `None` when no response is required.

past_due.title: Past Due
past_due.description: Whether the evidence submission deadline has passed.

order_id.title: Order Id
order_id.description: The ID of the order associated with the dispute.

payment_id.title: Payment Id
payment_id.description: The ID of the payment associated with the dispute.

RefundDispute.title: RefundDispute
RefundDispute.description:
Dispute associated with a refund,
in case we prevented a dispute by issuing a refund.

modified_at.title: Modified At
modified_at.description: Last modification timestamp of the object.

name.description: Human-friendly label for the connection, shown on the login page.

recurring_interval_count.description: Number of interval units of the subscription. If this is set to 1 the charge will happen every interval (e.g. every month), if set to 2 it will be every other month, and so on.

current_meter_period_start.title: Current Meter Period Start
current_meter_period_start.description: The start timestamp of the current meter period, if the product has a meter cycle set. Metered credits are granted and overage is settled on this cadence.

current_meter_period_end.title: Current Meter Period End
current_meter_period_end.description: The end timestamp of the current meter period, if the product has a meter cycle set. This is when credits next renew.

customer_cancellation_reason.description:
Customer reason for cancellation.

Helpful to monitor reasons behind churn for future improvements.

Only set this in case your own service is requesting the reason from the
customer. Or you know based on direct conversations, i.e support, with
the customer.

* `too_expensive`: Too expensive for the customer.
* `missing_features`: Customer is missing certain features.
* `switched_service`: Customer switched to another service.
* `unused`: Customer is not using it enough.
* `customer_service`: Customer is not satisfied with the customer service.
* `low_quality`: Customer is unhappy with the quality.
* `too_complex`: Customer considers the service too complicated.
* `other`: Other reason(s).

customer_cancellation_comment.title: Customer Cancellation Comment
customer_cancellation_comment.description:
Customer feedback and why they decided to cancel.

**IMPORTANT:**
Do not use this to store internal notes! It's intended to be input
from the customer and is therefore also available in their Polar
purchases library.

Only set this in case your own service is requesting the reason from the
customer. Or you copy a message directly from a customer
conversation, i.e support.

cancel_at_period_end.title: Cancel At Period End
cancel_at_period_end.description:
Cancel an active subscription once the current period ends.

Or uncancel a subscription currently set to be revoked at period end.

stops_collection.title: Stops Collection
stops_collection.description: Whether cancelling now also stops collecting the outstanding payment. True for a past-due subscription whose organization has no benefit revocation grace period: cancelling voids the pending order and stops dunning retries.

outstanding_amount.title: Outstanding Amount
outstanding_amount.description: Amount in cents still due on the pending order that would be voided, or null when nothing would be dropped.

SubscriptionCancelPreview.title: SubscriptionCancelPreview
SubscriptionCancelPreview.description: Preview of the effect of cancelling a subscription right now.

metered_amount.description: Total metered usage charges in cents (sum of all meter charges)

proration_amount.title: Proration Amount
proration_amount.description: Net pending proration adjustments in cents from mid-period changes (seat or product changes), to be billed on the next invoice.

net_amount.title: Net Amount
net_amount.description: Net amount in cents before taxes

total_amount.description: Total amount in cents (net + tax, before applying wallet balance)

applied_balance_amount.title: Applied Balance Amount
applied_balance_amount.description: Wallet balance applied to this charge in cents: negative when the customer's account credit reduces the amount due, positive when the charge clears outstanding debt, zero otherwise.

due_amount.title: Due Amount
due_amount.description: Amount actually due in cents: total_amount plus the applied wallet balance, floored at zero. This is the figure charged today.

SubscriptionChargePreview.description: Preview of the next charge for a subscription.

label.description: Human-readable description of the proration.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Signed amount in cents: positive to charge, negative to credit.

SubscriptionChargePreviewProration.description: A pending proration adjustment to be billed on the next invoice.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description:
Key-value object allowing you to store additional information.

The key must be a string with a maximum length of **40 characters**.
The value must be either:

* A string with a maximum length of **500 characters**
* An integer
* A floating-point number
* A boolean

You can store up to **50 key-value pairs**.

product_id.description: The ID of the recurring product to subscribe to. Must be a free product, otherwise the customer should go through a checkout flow.

customer_id.title: Customer Id
customer_id.description: The ID of the customer to create the subscription for.

discount_id.description: Update the subscription to apply a new discount. If set to `null`, the discount will be removed. The change will be applied on the next billing cycle.

trial_end.title: Trial End
trial_end.description: Set or extend the trial period of the subscription. If set to `now`, the trial will end immediately.

current_billing_period_end.title: Current Billing Period End
current_billing_period_end.description:
Set a new date for the end of the current billing period. The subscription will renew on this date. The new date can be earlier or later than the current period end, as long as it's in the future.

It is not possible to update the current billing period on a canceled subscription.

pending_update.title: Pending Update
pending_update.description: Clear the pending subscription update. Set to null to remove scheduled changes.

Known message types. Stored as a plain string column (not a DB enum):
``chat`` and lifecycle values are generic, while action values are
domain-specific and grow per case type. Validated at the app boundary.

TaxIDFormat.title: TaxIDFormat
TaxIDFormat.description:
List of supported tax ID formats.

Ref: https://docs.stripe.com/billing/customer/tax-ids#supported-tax-id

id.title: Id
id.description: Stable identifier for the jurisdiction. For US/Canada it is `{country}-{state}` (e.g. `US-CA`), otherwise the country code (e.g. `GB`).

state.title: State
state.description: ISO 3166-2 subdivision code, without the country prefix (e.g. `CA`). Only set for US and Canadian jurisdictions.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency of the remitted tax amount.

tax_amount.title: Tax Amount
tax_amount.description: Net tax remitted by Polar in this jurisdiction, in the currency's minor unit. Refunds and disputes are netted out.

order_count.title: Order Count
order_count.description: Number of orders that contributed tax to this jurisdiction.

state_name.title: State Name
state_name.description: Human-readable state/province name. Only set for US and Canadian jurisdictions.
